response.justification: The candidate is a versatile senior engineer with genuine language-tooling interests — they built C/C++ components for the LabWindows/CVI IDE at National Instruments and authored test runners and assertion libraries for DLang — but the core requirements of this role are not met. Java appears only peripherally in their history, there is no Kotlin experience at all, and no evidence of Spring or enterprise JVM framework work. Their recent decade has centered on TypeScript/Node.js web platforms and DLang, which is too far from deep JVM expertise for a mid-senior IDE tooling role.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI components)
Demonstrated interest in developer tooling — built test runners, assertion libraries, and MCP code generators
Strong software craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, workshops and teaching)
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only minor, dated Java exposure
No Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or enterprise JVM ecosystem experience
Recent work is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js), far from JVM internals
